agent_output:
```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with a sub-2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and refund policy for missed windows. Stakeholders have conflicting views on whether the time commitment is a guarantee or best-effort SLA.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery service with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee for express delivery service." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged regardless of delivery speed tier. Not refundable under express SLA miss." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period from order placement within which the express delivery is targeted for completion." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to physical pickup of the package by a driver." },
      { "term": "Window Miss", "definition": "An event where the express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, potentially triggering a goodwill refund of the express fee." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A discretionary (non-contractual) refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to eligibility conditions." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above, qualified to fulfill express delivery orders." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "An event where an assigned express driver abandons the delivery mid-fulfillment, triggering a replacement assignment process." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancellation within which a replacement driver must be assigned, or an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility Zone", "definition": "The 10km radius around a warehouse within which a customer's delivery address must fall to qualify for express delivery." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — a 2-hour window is operationally impossible beyond this radius. Allowing orders outside the zone corrupts the fulfillment model.",
      "testable_assertion": "An order with a delivery address >10km from the nearest warehouse MUST NOT be offered or accepted as express."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by commercial leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the total delivery charge equals base_delivery_fee + $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint — exceeding 15 minutes for pickup makes the 2-hour end-to-end window unachievable. This is a physical/logistical hard constraint, not a policy preference.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system MUST escalate (reassign, alert ops, or flag the order as at-risk)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Rating threshold is a quality policy that operations leadership could adjust.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system MUST NOT assign a driver with a rating below 4.0 to an express order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an express delivery window is missed, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund scope is a commercial policy decision, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A window-miss refund event MUST credit exactly the express fee amount and MUST NOT credit the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No goodwill refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund eligibility exclusion is a policy rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "If the order is flagged with 'incorrect_address', a window-miss refund MUST NOT be issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Replacement time target is an operational SLA, not a physical constraint. Ops could adjust this threshold.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancellation event occurs on an express order, the system MUST attempt reassignment. If no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the system MUST trigger an automatic refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express customers must have real-time tracking visibility of their assigned driver.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "UX feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "While an express order is in 'in_transit' status, the customer-facing interface MUST display live driver location updated at least every 30 seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the nearest warehouse (boundary condition).", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is the 10km threshold inclusive or exclusive? Needs clarification. Recommend inclusive (<=10km)." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars mid-delivery (e.g., previous delivery's rating arrives).", "expected_behavior": "The current express delivery should be completed by the assigned driver. The rating gate applies at assignment time, not during fulfillment." },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels within the 10-minute replacement window.", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does the 10-minute clock reset per cancellation or is it cumulative? If cumulative, a second cancellation at minute 8 gives only 2 minutes. Needs policy decision." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to a location >10km from warehouse.", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the order be downgraded to standard and express fee refunded? Or should the address change be rejected?" },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers available at time of order placement.", "expected_behavior": "Express option should not be offered to the customer, or the order should be queued with a clear warning. Needs clarification on whether express should be hidden or shown-but-disabled." },
    { "scenario": "Order placed at 11:55 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight and potentially crosses into non-operating hours.", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — are there operating hours for express delivery? Notes are silent on this." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a correct but ambiguous address (e.g., missing apartment number) causing delay.", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — Finance says no refund for 'wrong address' but this is a gray area between wrong and incomplete." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills?", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — notes don't specify warehouse selection logic. Nearest? Inventory-based? Load-balanced?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14 (within 15-min SLA) but delivery completes at 2h01m.", "expected_behavior": "This is a window miss — refund eligibility applies per BR-5/BR-6 regardless of pickup timing." },
    { "scenario": "Network outage prevents real-time tracking during express delivery.", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — does loss of tracking visibility trigger any SLA implications or customer notification?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee at $12.99 creates a premium revenue stream. Refund exposure on window misses is a direct cost — if miss rates exceed ~15-20%, the product becomes margin-negative. The 3-business-day refund processing window preserves short-term cash flow but may drive chargebacks if customers perceive delays.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the eligible driver pool. Express orders will compete with standard orders for high-rated drivers, potentially degrading standard delivery performance. The 10-minute replacement window after driver cancellation requires either a warm standby pool or very fast matching algorithms.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and 2-hour window are strong value propositions. However, the legal vs. marketing contradiction on 'guarantee' language creates trust risk — if customers pay $12.99 expecting a guarantee and Legal disclaims it, CSAT will suffer on misses.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: The contradiction between 'guaranteed' (PM/CS) and 'best effort' (Legal) is a compliance and brand risk. Marketing a 'guarantee' that Legal won't honor could trigger consumer protection scrutiny. SECONDARY RISK: The 'incorrect address' refund exclusion requires a fair, auditable process to determine fault — without it, this becomes a customer dispute magnet.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Pay premium for faster delivery. Confusing guarantee language could erode trust. Real-time tracking adds transparency." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "High-rated drivers face increased demand and tighter time pressure. 4-star gate creates a two-tier driver economy." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Ops", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA requires express orders to be prioritized in picking/packing queues, potentially delaying standard orders." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around window misses, 'incorrect address' determinations, and refund timing. Need clear playbooks." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Refund liability tracking, 3-business-day processing SLA, revenue recognition for express fees with conditional refund." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must ensure all customer-facing language avoids enforceable guarantee claims. Refund terms must be disclosed pre-purchase." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is low due to a direct contradiction between PM/Customer Success ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / goodwill gesture') on the core product promise. This is not a minor detail — it defines the fundamental nature of the product, the refund obligation model, and all customer-facing language. Additionally, the refund trigger mechanism is contradictory: CS says automatic refund on failed replacement, Finance implies a manual 3-business-day process. Until these contradictions are resolved at the leadership level, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99)",
      "10km eligibility zone constraint",
      "15-minute pickup SLA",
      "4-star minimum driver rating",
      "Base delivery fee is non-refundable on window miss"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA (PM vs Legal contradiction)",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or manually processed (CS vs Finance contradiction)",
      "What constitutes an 'incorrect address' for refund exclusion purposes",
      "Operating hours and time-boundary behavior",
      "Warehouse selection logic when multiple are in range"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance — needs confirmation",
      "Driver rating is the cumulative all-time rating, not a rolling window — needs confirmation",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "Express delivery applies to all product categories — no size/weight/hazmat exclusions mentioned"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' with refund obligations. Legal explicitly states this is 'best effort' and the refund is a non-enforceable 'goodwill gesture.' These are fundamentally incompatible framings of the core product promise. This must be resolved before any implementation — it affects UI copy, refund automation logic, terms of service, and customer communication templates.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Treat as guarantee — refund is an obligation, UI says 'guaranteed', Legal updates ToS to reflect binding commitment",
        "B: Treat as best-effort — UI says 'express delivery target', refund is discretionary, no automation required",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Promise' (not 'guarantee'), automate refunds as goodwill but include ToS disclaimer that it is not a contractual right"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — the hybrid approach. It gives customers a strong value proposition without creating legal exposure. Automate the refund to preserve trust, but frame it in ToS as discretionary. This is standard practice in logistics (cf. Amazon same-day, UPS Express guarantees with force majeure carveouts)."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refunds should be 'automatic' on failed driver replacement. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These describe fundamentally different refund mechanisms — one is system-triggered and instant, the other is a manual back-office process with a processing window.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Fully automatic — system triggers instant refund on window miss, Finance adjusts process to match",
        "B: Auto-approved, batch-processed — system flags refund immediately but Finance processes in daily batches (1-3 day delay)",
        "C: Manual review — all refunds go to a queue for human approval within 3 business days"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — auto-approve the refund at the moment of window miss (customer sees 'refund approved' immediately) but process the actual credit in Finance's standard batch cycle. This satisfies CS's need for customer-facing automation and Finance's need for process control."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The definition of 'incorrect address' for refund exclusion is undefined. There is a spectrum from clearly wrong (non-existent street) to ambiguous (missing apartment number, gated community with no access code). Without a clear taxonomy, this will become the #1 source of customer disputes and inconsistent support decisions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Strict — any address issue blocks refund",
        "B: Narrow — only verifiably non-existent or unreachable addresses block refund",
        "C: Driver-attested — driver marks 'address issue' with photo proof, support reviews"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — require driver attestation with evidence. This is auditable, fair, and defensible. Pure driver word-of-mouth (without evidence) will be gamed."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours defined for express delivery. The 2-hour window could span midnight, holidays, or low-driver-availability periods. Also missing: product category restrictions (weight, size, hazmat), maximum orders per warehouse per hour (capacity caps), and what happens to express orders during system outages or extreme weather.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Ops", "Drivers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express available 24/7 with dynamic availability based on driver supply",
        "B: Express available during defined hours only (e.g., 7AM-10PM)",
        "C: Express availability controlled per-warehouse based on ops capacity"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — per-warehouse availability gives ops the most control and avoids promising express when fulfillment capacity doesn't exist. Start with defined hours (Option B) and evolve to dynamic (Option A) once demand patterns are understood."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define acceptance criteria when the core product promise (guarantee vs. best-effort) is contradicted between stakeholders.", "business_rule_broken": "The nature of BR-5 (refund on window miss) depends entirely on whether this is an obligation or discretionary — which is unresolved." },
    { "violation": "Refund mechanism is contradictory (automatic vs. 3-business-day manual process) — cannot specify refund flow behavior.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-5 and BR-7 refund triggers cannot be implemented until the automation vs. manual process contradiction is resolved." }
  ]
}
```
